title: Economy: why Poland is booming while eastern Germany falls behind
content: Eastern Germany risks falling behind, economists warn, while neighbours such as Poland are booming. What sets them apart, and what does the East need to turn things around?
snippet: Eastern Germany risks falling behind, economists warn, while neighbours such as Poland are booming. What sets them apart, and what does the East need to turn things around?

title: Paris Saint-Germain wins the Champions League after penalty shootout victory against Arsenal
content: Paris Saint-Germain won back-to-back Champions League titles after a 4–3 penalty shootout victory over Arsenal in Budapest. Following a 1–1 draw through extra time, Gabriel Magalhães missed the decisive spot kick to hand PSG the trophy.

title: Austrian protesters shut Brenner motorway on Saturday over pollution
content: Thousands of demonstrators blocked Austria’s vital Brenner motorway on Saturday to protest rising traffic noise and pollution. The eight-hour shutdown of the transit corridor between Germany and Italy disrupted travel.
snippet: Thousands of demonstrators blocked Austria’s vital Brenner motorway on Saturday to protest rising traffic noise and pollution. The eight-hour shutdown of the transit corridor between Germany and Italy

title: Galați residents fear future incidents after Russian drone crashed into building
content: A Russian drone crashed into a residential building into the eastern Romanian city of Galați on Friday, sparking a fire to its roof and injuring two people.
snippet: A Russian drone crashed into a residential building into the eastern Romanian city of Galați on Friday, sparking a fire to its roof and injuring two people.

title: Maltese cast ballots as Labour government seeks fourth term in snap election
content: Maltese voters headed to the polls on Saturday for a snap election, with Prime Minister Robert Abela’s Labour Party tipped to win on a platform of economic stability and 4% GDP growth, despite ongoing corruption concerns.

title: Polish President Nawrocki seeks to strip Zelenskyy of Poland's highest honour
content: President Karol Nawrocki said he will ask for Ukrainian President Volodymyr Zelenskyy to be stripped of the White Eagle, after Zelenskyy named a military unit after a group that led massacres of ethnic Poles during WWII.

title: France sees as sharp rise in anti-Jewish, anti-Christian and anti-Muslim acts, says government
content: In a new report, the French Interior Ministry says that "anti-religious acts strike at freedom of conscience and the free exercise of religion" and endanger "the very balance of our society".
snippet: In a new report, the French Interior Ministry says that "anti-religious acts strike at freedom of conscience and the free exercise of religion" and endanger "the very balance of our society".

title: France launches an inquiry into the treatment of activists on the Gaza flotilla

title: The drone crash is “the full responsibility of the Russian Federation”, says Romanian FM
content: Oana Toiu, Romania's foreign minister, confirmed to Euronews that the drone that struck Romania was of Russian origin and was carrying explosives.
snippet: Oana Toiu, Romania's foreign minister, confirmed to Euronews that the drone that struck Romania was of Russian origin and was carrying explosives.

title: Four EU countries push Brussels to ease carbon market pressure on industry
content: Estonia, France, Germany and Spain support the Emissions Trading System, the EU’s major climate policy tool, but want clearer, simpler and possibly more flexible rules to protect industrial competitiveness, according to a document seen by Euronews.

title: French parliament inches towards symbolic repeal of 'Code noir' slavery legislation
content: France abolished enslaving humans more than 170 years ago and in 2001 recognised slavery and the slave trade as "crimes against humanity."
snippet: France abolished enslaving humans more than 170 years ago and in 2001 recognised slavery and the slave trade as "crimes against humanity."

title: Top-ranked Jannik Sinner crashes out of French Open after apparent struggle with heat
content: Sinner received medical attention and left the court and minerals were added to his drink when he returned but he wasn't able to continue.
snippet: Sinner received medical attention and left the court and minerals were added to his drink when he returned but he wasn't able to continue.

title: Brussels faces backlash over carbon trading system as production costs soar
content: Several EU countries argue that planned cuts to free emissions allowances under the EU Emissions Trading System are too severe and do not reflect the technological limits faced by sectors such as steel, cement, chemicals and aluminium.

title: EU seeks to boost Europe’s chip demand in tech sovereignty bid
content: The EU's revamped Chips Act shifts focus from supply to demand to boost European chip demand, especially for AI. The draft law covers demand aggregation and crisis tools, as part of a broader tech sovereignty push also covering cloud services and open source.

title: Italian police seize more than €200m in late mafia boss Matteo Messina Denaro's assets
content: Assets were being seized in Andorra, the Cayman Islands, Gibraltar, Lebanon, Luxembourg, Monaco, Spain and Switzerland, as well as Italy, police said.
snippet: Assets were being seized in Andorra, the Cayman Islands, Gibraltar, Lebanon, Luxembourg, Monaco, Spain and Switzerland, as well as Italy, police said.

title: Russia launches fresh strikes on Ukraine, Kyiv hits refinery hub

title: Malta's Labour party wins historic fourth term: preliminary count

title: Accounts linked to EU-sanctioned figures displayed monetisation tools after sanctions, report finds

title: Romanian foreign minister: Russia bears full responsibility for drone strike
content: In an exclusive interview, Romania Foreign minister Oana Țoiu said it’s “Russia’s full responsibility” that civilians in Romania were harmed after a Russian drone hit a residential building in the country. Țoiu also said they had "final confirmation" that the drone "was carrying explosives".

title: What we know about the Geran-2 drone that hit a residential building in Romania
content: The Iranian-made Shahed drones are one of Russia's main long-range strike UAV’s used by Moscow against Ukraine. First deployed back in 2022, Russia then set up domestic production, calling them Geran.
snippet: The Iranian-made Shahed drones are one of Russia's main long-range strike UAV’s used by Moscow against Ukraine. First deployed back in 2022, Russia then set up domestic production, calling them Geran.

content: Security Action for Europe (SAFE) is a loan instrument designed to provide financial support to EU member states to speed up defence readiness.

title: Hungary unlocks €16.4bn in EU funds after Magyar secures deal with Brussels

title: The German village that made itself immune to the Hormuz shock
content: When the Hormuz crisis sent energy prices up across Europe, most households had little choice but to absorb the shock. The residents of Feldheim, a small village in eastern Germany, barely noticed. They pay just 12 cents per kilowatt-hour for electricity.
snippet: When the Hormuz crisis sent energy prices up across Europe, most households had little choice but to absorb the shock. The residents of Feldheim, a small village in eastern Germany, barely noticed.
